content:
Our department is shrinking and we're being faced with doing more work with less people, effectively broadening the scope of the work each of us is involved with. For me, this seems to increases the importance of having good documentation so we can quickly come up to speed on something when we haven't touched it in a while. At the same time, we have less time to write documentation than ever before.
Are there AI tools or particular flows that you've found effective for quickly creating well-formatted documentation? I'm imagining something where I could dictate or dump all of my thoughts in a stream of consciousness and let the AI organize them in a helpful way (that I would then vet of course). This is less about the technical documentation for something like an API, and more about "what the heck was this thing even for, and why did we build it?"
Much thanks for any input.

  submitted by   /u/launchoverittt
[link]   [comments]
